title: Tithymalus philorus
intro: Tithymalus philorus, also known as Euphorbia pilorus, is a species of spurge plant in the Euphorbiaceae family, characterized by its succulent stems and leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and skin conditions like acne and eczema. The bioactive constituents of Tithymalus philorus include euphorbin, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as tinctures and infusions, often combined with other medicinal plants, to treat various health conditions.

uses: Tithymalus philorus, commonly known as the 'milkweed frothplant', has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, although its effectiveness is largely anecdotal.
